claim: Hand B plant markers (_.e.e, _.eeo, _.o.eo) ride on closed consonant-stem classes analogous to Hand A's _.oii class (which rides the closed class {kn, sn, dn, tkn} accounting for 33 of 36 tokens at >90% coverage per H-BV-CONSONANT-ID-01). If Hand B markers are similarly closed-class, this supports the category-marker interpretation; if Hand B markers use open vocabulary, they encode something structurally different from Hand A _.oii.
evidence_for: H-BV-CONSONANT-ID-01 refutation showed _.oii rides {kn, sn, dn} at 29/36 tokens, with trailing {tkn, kkn, dr} making up 35/36 — a closed 5-6 element class at >95% coverage
If the two hands use analogous closed-class marker systems, it supports the two-scribes-same-encoding framing of H-BV-HAND-B-PLANT-01
Closed lexical class is the expected form of a category tag in a morphologically productive system
evidence_against: Hand B has 42 total _.e.e + _.eeo + _.o.eo tokens on plant folios; with three distinct vowel patterns, the closed-class test runs on smaller sub-samples
Hand B's vocabulary is more balanced (o/e ratio 1.36 vs Hand A 2.00) which may imply broader stem coverage
If Hand B has open-class markers, the scribe-dialect story needs refining — same FUNCTION (plant mark) but different LEXICAL STRUCTURE across scribes
test: For each of the three Hand-B plant-marker vowel patterns (_.e.e, _.eeo, _.o.eo), extract every Hand-B plant-folio word exactly matching it. Record the consonant skeleton for each. Count unique skeletons per marker. Report whether the top-5 skeletons account for >80% of tokens (CLOSED) or not (OPEN). Cross-hand comparison: do any Hand-B marker skeletons appear in Hand A's {kn, sn, dn, tkn, kkn, dr} set?
test_threshold: For each marker pattern: top-5 skeletons account for >80% of tokens = CLOSED CLASS. <80% = OPEN. After per-marker classification: if all three markers are CLOSED, overall verdict = CLOSED (supports shared closed-class framing). If any marker is OPEN, the overall verdict is MIXED or OPEN.

tests_run.details: All 3 Hand-B markers OPEN: _.e.e top-5 75.0%, _.eeo 73.3%, _.o.eo 72.7% (all below 80%% threshold). 25 unique skeletons across 42 tokens (60%% unique-per-token). Hand A _.oii by contrast has 6 skeletons across 36 tokens (17%%). Cross-hand overlap: 0 shared stems. Hypothesis refuted — Hand B does NOT use analogous closed class.

tests_remaining: Execute the locked test in a separate commit after this pre-registration
Cross-hand stem overlap analysis regardless of primary verdict
reasoning: Extends the closed-class observation from Hand A to Hand B. The pre-registered threshold (top-5 stems > 80% coverage) is the operational definition of 'closed class' that matches Hand A's actual observed distribution. If Hand B behaves the same way, the scribe-dialect framing of H-BV-HAND-B-PLANT-01 generalises from 'different markers' to 'different closed-class vocabularies for the same function.'
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 pre-registration after H-BV-CONSONANT-ID-01 refutation revealed Hand-A _.oii closed-class structure
